metadata.methodology: Serious Further Offences are crimes committed by people on probation supervision that result in a conviction for a specified serious offence. Vacancy rate is from HMPPS workforce statistics. Caseload per officer is calculated from Offender Management Statistics divided by HM Prison and Probation Service staff census.
knownIssues: SFO figures reflect convictions, not offences — delay between offence and conviction means figures may understate current risk
Vacancy rate definition changed in 2021 following reunification of probation service; pre/post not directly comparable
Average caseload figures are national means; some officers carry significantly higher caseloads
